The question is asking to determine which island has the most area when the system must treat the winner as both larger and smaller than the other, while simultaneously maximizing and minimizing the same area metric. To handle contradictory conditions, we cannot perform a straightforward sort. Instead, we need to understand that this scenario is impossible to fulfill with normal sorting because it requires a single item to be both the largest and smallest at the same time. Therefore, the question seems to describe a paradoxical situation where traditional sorting cannot provide a meaningful answer.

judge_thought: The question is inherently contradictory and paradoxical. It asks for an island that must simultaneously be both larger and smaller than another island, while also requiring the system to maximize and minimize the same area metric. This is logically impossible, as an island cannot be both larger and smaller than another at the same time, nor can a single area metric be both maximized and minimized simultaneously. Thus, there is no valid answer to this question.
